sections.analysis_flow: read-only bounded audit (existing experiment outputs already answer the question; no new slice was launched).
sections.comparison_target: Accepted FA4 paper roofline baseline predictor (artifact-ACCEPTED_BASELINE_RECORD-e673ec2be9b4) evaluated on the same held-out validation split and emulator-based ground truth.
sections.evidence_question: Does the combined white-box predictor satisfy the research goal of predicting FA4 kernel runtime and bottleneck from the input configuration alone, and what are the dominant mechanisms, caveats, and next actions?
sections.parent_claim: A white-box forward-pass predictor that models occupancy-limited active warps, effective HBM/L2/TMA bandwidth, and precision-specific MMA/MUFU throughput will achieve held-out MAPE <= 25% and beat the FA4 paper roofline baseline by at least 5 percentage points MAPE on the B200 held-out validation set (artifact-SELECTED_HYPOTHESIS-bab659036a3c).
sections.stop_condition: The analysis stage ends when the empirical pass has a documented interpretation, bottleneck diagnosis, limitation statement, and a recommended next macro route.

summary: Read-only audit of the completed FA4 B200 experiment, interpreting whether the combined white-box predictor satisfies the goal of predicting kernel runtime from input configuration without executing the kernel.
title: Analysis Context Brief: FA4 B200 Combined White-Box Predictor
